intro_station_master.speaker: Unknown Voice
intro_station_master.text: You found the wrong berth on the worst possible day. Good news: the ship still flies. Bad news: half the station wants you dead, and the other half wants the bounty.

choices.label: Then we take the ship and leave.
I want details before I trust a whisper in the dark.

intro_station_master_who.speaker: Unknown Voice
intro_station_master_who.text: Call me the berth ghost. I kept this crate hidden until someone meaner than me started looking for it. That happened ten minutes ago.

effects.log: The hidden berth unlocks with a shriek of old seals.

intro_station_master_take.speaker: Unknown Voice
intro_station_master_take.text: Finally. Grab the salvage tech by the coolant stack if you want the engines lit before security drills through the hull. And if she mouths off, that means she likes you.

intro_station_master_detail.speaker: Unknown Voice
intro_station_master_detail.text: The ship was used to smuggle pre-collapse relics, living cargo, and one thing nobody names out loud. Whoever controlled it disappeared. Whoever finds it next gets hunted.

intro_station_master_fail.speaker: Unknown Voice
intro_station_master_fail.text: You can doubt me on your own time. Right now, boots are coming down the catwalk and those people do not negotiate.

vexa_intro.speaker: Vexa Sorn
vexa_intro.text: Let me guess. You touched the cursed ship, made eye contact with death, and now you're hoping I'm the kind of idiot who can fix both. Annoying for you: I actually can.

choices.label: Join me and keep us alive.

vexa_join.speaker: Vexa Sorn
vexa_join.text: Fine. I get a bunk, first pick of interesting scrap, and veto power if you try to fly this coffin without enough coolant again.

vexa_notyet.speaker: Vexa Sorn
vexa_notyet.text: Cute speech. Try again after you survive one firefight and stop smelling like coolant panic.

vexa_ship.speaker: Vexa Sorn
vexa_ship.text: Core bleed, fuel slosh, vent ghosts, and a nav ghost that either needs prayer or a soldering gun. Mostly fixable. Probably.

vexa_flirt.speaker: Vexa Sorn
vexa_flirt.text: Don't flirt with me while the station is trying to kill us. Or do. I respect terrible timing.

choices.label: I'll save the line for later.

talon_intro.speaker: Talon Rhyse
talon_intro.text: Your ship landed in the middle of my problem. Lucky for you, I don't charge rescue rates until the second incident.

choices.label: Help me, and I'll get you off this moon.
You look like you enjoy violence. Need work?
Nice armor. Loot it from a corpse or a bad idea?

talon_test.speaker: Talon Rhyse
talon_test.text: Not convinced. Kill the raider boss up the ridge and we can revisit your recruitment pitch.

talon_join.speaker: Talon Rhyse
talon_join.text: Fine. But if your command style is 'improvise and bleed,' say so now and save me the surprise.

choices.label: Bleeding is part of the vibe.

talon_flirt.speaker: Talon Rhyse
talon_flirt.text: Depends which piece of armor you're admiring. Careful, commander. I might assume that's a line.

dock_scav_intro.speaker: Dax Renn
dock_scav_intro.text: If you are here to loot the berth, get in line. If you are here to survive, the vents still have a way through. The corpses figured that out too late.

choices.label: Seen a ledger or a civic burn order?

dock_scav_vents.speaker: Dax Renn
dock_scav_vents.text: Debtor lockers, coolant drip, one or two desperate runners. There is a hidden crawl entrance in the northwest catwalk rail if you do not mind grime and regret.

dock_scav_ledger.speaker: Dax Renn
dock_scav_ledger.text: A fixer named Naia Voss has been buying up debt slates before civic can torch them. If there is money in your future, she is sniffing around the south gantry.

dock_scav_repeat.speaker: Dax Renn
dock_scav_repeat.text: The vents are ugly, the fixers are liars, and the dead are not getting less dead. That is my update.

civic_enforcer_intro.speaker: Officer Merik
civic_enforcer_intro.text: This dock is under civic hold. Turn around, keep your hands visible, and maybe I pretend I never saw your face. Maybe.

effects.log: Officer Merik reaches for his sidearm.

civic_enforcer_warn.speaker: Officer Merik
civic_enforcer_warn.text: Then pass faster. And stay out of the restricted lane unless you enjoy paperwork and blood loss in equal measure.

civic_enforcer_threat.speaker: Officer Merik
civic_enforcer_threat.text: Wrong answer. One more bad choice and this becomes an arrest.

civic_enforcer_info.speaker: Officer Merik
civic_enforcer_info.text: Smuggler berth popped open, scavenger killers moved in, civic got blamed for not seeing it sooner. Now everyone is armed and offended.

civic_enforcer_repeat.speaker: Officer Merik
civic_enforcer_repeat.text: Move along, commander. My patience is not a public utility.

naia_intro.speaker: Naia Voss
naia_intro.text: You look like someone who needs credits before their luck finishes rotting. I need a ledger pulled from the lower vents before civic ash-cans it.

effects.log: Naia marks a vent route on your pad.

naia_terms.speaker: Naia Voss
naia_terms.text: Two hundred credits, one favor later, and the satisfaction of ruining the right person's week. The locker is tagged RED-9 in the vent maze.

naia_repeat.speaker: Naia Voss
naia_repeat.text: RED-9. Lower vents. Do not let civic cremate the paper before I do something profitable with it.

naia_ledger_turnin.speaker: Naia Voss
naia_ledger_turnin.text: There it is. Half the station owes me for this little slab of misery. Hand it over and I pay on the spot.

effects.log: Naia transfers the credits without smiling.

vent_runner_intro.speaker: Orin Pike
vent_runner_intro.text: Easy! I am not armed enough to die over a locker. You are after RED-9 too, right? Civic already turned this place over once.

choices.label: Relax. I just need the ledger.

vent_runner_info.speaker: Orin Pike
vent_runner_info.text: There is a civic burn order attached to that slate. Whoever held the debts did not want them surviving the week. Also, something in the back tunnel hates light.

vent_runner_backoff.speaker: Orin Pike
vent_runner_backoff.text: Oh, gladly. Heroism is for people with replacement organs.

vent_runner_share.speaker: Orin Pike
vent_runner_share.text: Fine by me. If you get out alive, tell Naia she still owes me a finder's cut.

wake_drone_intro.speaker: Cargo Drone L3M
wake_drone_intro.text: Shipboard inventory advisory: unsecured freight, sealed smuggler hold, and one reactor bypass key hidden by someone with trust issues.

wake_drone_key.speaker: Cargo Drone L3M
wake_drone_key.text: Bypass key registered to Hold Locker B. The compartment was not on the official manifest, which means it is definitely interesting.

wake_drone_manifest.speaker: Cargo Drone L3M
wake_drone_manifest.text: Manifest includes ration crates, dry coolant coils, contraband spice, and three entries redacted so aggressively I am offended.

wake_drone_repeat.speaker: Cargo Drone L3M
wake_drone_repeat.text: Reminder: unsecured freight becomes floor freight during hard turns. This has been a safety notice.

ember_scout_intro.speaker: Sera Flint
ember_scout_intro.text: You are not from Hollow. Good. Everyone local is either desperate or pretending not to be. Watch the ridge — Krail runs spotters before he runs raids.

ember_scout_krail.speaker: Sera Flint
ember_scout_krail.text: Ash raider boss. Mean, superstitious, and convinced your ship came here for him personally. He has a fence working the camps too.

choices.label: I will keep that in mind.

ember_scout_help.speaker: Sera Flint
ember_scout_help.text: You can start by killing fewer miners than the raiders do. After that, we improvise.

ember_scout_repeat.speaker: Sera Flint
ember_scout_repeat.text: Sand in the lungs, raiders on the ridge, and strangers with ships asking questions. Same ugly day.

glassreef_watcher_repeat.speaker: Reef Watcher
glassreef_watcher_repeat.text: The old transit hum has changed since you arrived. That is either hopeful or catastrophic.

archivist_intro.speaker: Archivist Pell
archivist_intro.text: If you touched anything glowing on the way in, please tell me now so I can worry in the correct direction. The archive has survived worse visitors, but not by much.

choices.label: I found an idol in the flooded stacks.

archivist_place.speaker: Archivist Pell
archivist_place.text: Glassreef kept moving even after the city drowned. These records tell us who, what, and sometimes why. Mostly they tell us how bad the old world lied.

archivist_idol.speaker: Archivist Pell
archivist_idol.text: That transit idol should be in a sealed niche, which means someone opened the wrong chamber or the chamber opened itself. Neither theory is restful.

effects.log: Pell pays for the idol and shares a troubling transit record.

archivist_work.speaker: Archivist Pell
archivist_work.text: There is always work if you can tolerate mildew, ghosts in the transit rails, and other people's secrets.

archivist_repeat.speaker: Archivist Pell
archivist_repeat.text: If you find any pre-collapse transit seals, bring them dry and unbroken. I am tired of reconstructing history from mud.
